track.label: Line-oriented / regular-expression architecture
track.hypothesis: Physical-line assembly plus anchored regular-expression field tokenization can robustly parse the vendor dialect while producing precise errors without becoming a whole-input CSV state machine.
track.final_decision: Promote loop 02: all 38 expanded objective tests and all blocking core gates pass.

iterations.hypothesis: Regex physical-line splitting and field tokenization, with minimal quote-balance scanning, will cover the dialect and establish an objective baseline.
iterations.outcome: Ran 18 tests: 17 passed and 1 failed. All three blocking core samples passed, but post-closing-quote junk was reported at line 2, column 1 instead of the offending column 4, so the objective gate failed.

commands.build: Create loop-01/parser.py and loop-01/test_parser.py using line splitting, quote-balance assembly, and anchored field regexes.
commands.judge: Inspect loop-01/test-result.txt, apply the five-criterion weighted scorecard, and enforce core/objective gates.

presentation.caption: 18 run, 17 passed, 1 failed; core gates passed.
Scorecard assessment and actual failure-driven next hypothesis.

scores.notes: Core behavior passed, but the objective location assertion failed.
Broad baseline coverage with generic malformed-token diagnosis.
Line and column were present but the failed case pointed at the token start.
Compact, focused helpers and regular expressions.
Genuinely line/regex oriented with no forbidden parser import.

prompt.track_prompt: Implement the specified messy CSV parser under the line-oriented/regular-expression architecture and run exactly two evidence-bearing loops.
prompt.input_feedback: No prior candidate; establish the objective baseline.
prompt.judge_feedback: Core gates passed, but closing-quote junk was localized to column 1 rather than column 4.
prompt.next_prompt: Retain regex tokenization; add precise line-local structural diagnostics and source-offset mapping, then expand adversarial malformed checks.

lesson.trigger: A regex token match failed at the field boundary after a closing quote.
lesson.action: Diagnose quote structure while physical lines are assembled and retain exact source offsets.
lesson.evidence: The only failed test expected line 2, column 4; the generic fallback emitted line 2, column 1.

iterations.stop_reason: Primary objective tests failed, although blocking core samples passed.

iterations.hypothesis: A line-local structural quote validator and source-offset map will fix precise diagnostics while preserving regex field tokenization and all valid behavior.
iterations.outcome: Ran 38 expanded tests: all 38 passed. The loop-01 failure is fixed, all blocking core samples pass, and multiline CRLF error locations remain physical and precise.

commands.build: Improve loop-02/parser.py with line-local quote validation and logical-record source maps; expand test_parser.py and add tests/fixtures/valid-messy.csv.
commands.judge: Inspect loop-02/test-result.txt, compare against loop 01, apply the weighted scorecard, and enforce core/objective gates.

presentation.caption: 38 run, 38 passed; core and objective gates passed.
Comparison, provisional scores, and promotion rationale.

scores.notes: All 38 objective tests and every blocking sample passed.
Expanded valid and malformed boundaries include mixed newlines, Unicode whitespace, and multiline coordinates.
Specific categories and precise 1-based physical locations passed adversarial assertions.
Validation state remains isolated to logical-record assembly and helpers remain focused.
Field tokenization remains regex based; no csv, pandas, or third-party parser is used.

prompt.track_prompt: Improve the loop-01 line/regex parser from measured failures and expand malformed/error-location evidence.
prompt.input_feedback: Loop 01 passed 17/18 tests and every core gate, but localized closing-quote junk to the field start.
prompt.judge_feedback: Loop 02 passes all 38 tests, fixes the measured location failure, and preserves architecture constraints.
prompt.next_prompt: Stop after the mandated second loop and promote this snapshot; seek new vendor fixtures before adding complexity.

lesson.trigger: Normalized CRLF content and regex fallback positions can diverge from physical source coordinates.
lesson.action: Carry a source-offset map while assembling each logical record and validate quote boundaries on each physical line.
lesson.evidence: The expanded 38-test suite passes post-CRLF line/column assertions and the original column-4 failure.

iterations.stop_reason: Completed exactly two mandated loops with all core and objective gates passing.
